---
title: "Part IV — Mechanical, Vacuum, Cryogenics & Optics"
---

*The apparatus itself: how to design mechanisms that stay put, pump down to UHV, get cold, and point a laser where you want it.*

| Module | Topic | Tier highlight |
|--------|-------|---------------|
| M18 | Precision mechanical design | Exact constraint / kinematic design |
| M19 | Materials selection | Cryogenic property changes |
| M20 | Manufacturing and tolerances | GD&T, tolerance stack-up |
| M21 | Vibration isolation | Transmissibility and active isolation |
| M22 | Vacuum technology | Pump types, CF flanges, leak detection |
| M23 | Cryogenics | Dilution refrigerators, wiring heat loads |
| M24 | Thermometry and temperature control | Cernox, 4-wire, PID coupling |
| M25 | Optics and lasers | Two-mirror walk, Gaussian beams |
| M26 | Magnetics | Superconducting magnets, quench |

**Key references for Part IV:** @mooreDavisCoplan2009, @smithChetwynd1992, @slocum1992precision, @ohanlon2003vacuum, @pobell2007matter, @ekin2006experimental, @venturaRisegari2008.
